tool.name: Cross product
tool.description: Cross two list collections into a nested list:list collection
tool.command: mkdir -p xprod && ## Build TSV manifests of <element_identifier>\t<path> for both input lists ## by iterating each collection via Cheetah. #for $el in $anchors printf '%s\t%s\n' '$el.element_identifier' '$el' >> anchors.tsv && #end for #for $el in $queries printf '%s\t%s\n' '$el.element_identifier' '$el' >> queries.tsv && #end for python '$__tool_directory__/cross_product.py' --anchors anchors.tsv --queries queries.tsv --outdir xprod --ext dat $include_self

param.name: anchors
param.label: Anchor list collection (outer dimension)
param.help: A 'list' collection; each element identifier becomes an OUTER element of the result.

param.name: queries
param.label: Query list collection (inner dimension)
param.help: A 'list' collection; each element identifier becomes an INNER element under every anchor. The query file is what is carried into the result.

param.name: include_self
param.label: Include self-pairs (anchor == query)
param.help: By default an anchor is never crossed with the query of the same element identifier.

tool.help:
**Cross product** — build a `list:list` collection from two `list` collections.
Given an **anchor** `list` collection (outer dimension) and a **query** `list` collection (inner dimension), this tool enumerates every `(anchor, query)` combination and emits a **`list:list`** collection:
- outer element identifier = `{anchor}` - inner element identifier = `{query}` - the dataset held at `[{anchor}][{query}]` is the **query**'s file.
By default, the combination where `anchor == query` (matched on element identifier) is **excluded**, so a reference assembly is never projected onto itself. Enable *Include self-pairs* to keep them.
-----
**Why this exists**
Several pipeline steps are an anchor × query cross-product that a pure gxformat2 map-over cannot enumerate:
- **WF-C C.4** — project each anchor's annotation onto every *other* assembly. - **WF-K per-assembly fanout** — emit a per-(reference, target) directed structure for the hub.
Producing the `list:list` up front turns those into a single map-over-able collection: map over the **outer** dimension to act per-anchor, or over the inner to act per-pair.
-----
**Inputs**
- *Anchor list collection* — `list`; identifiers become OUTER elements. - *Query list collection* — `list`; identifiers become INNER elements; the query file is what is carried into each `[anchor][query]` cell. - *Include self-pairs* — keep `anchor == query` (default off).
**Output**
- A `list:list` collection. With A anchors and Q queries and the default self-exclusion, each anchor contributes `Q` inner elements, minus one when an anchor identifier also appears among the queries.
-----
**Implementation note**
The wrapper iterates each input collection to write `<identifier>\t<path>` manifests, then a small bundled Python helper (`cross_product.py`, stdlib only) symlinks each query file as `{anchor}__{query}.dat` into a flat directory. Galaxy's `discover_datasets` two-group pattern `(?P<identifier_0>.+?)__(?P<identifier_1>.+)\.dat` then nests them into the `list:list` structure (identifiers must not contain the `__` separator).
Source: authored for the Pv4 pangenome pipeline.
